independence.approach: After drafting, a separate checklist-driven pass reopened the foundation, all seven revision-bound source snapshots and records, retained rights evidence, structured media provenance, generated attribution, and every learner-facing claim. The pass recalculated representative examples, tested theorem boundaries, challenged error-correction overclaims, and verified license compatibility without relying on drafting notes.

checks.note: FOUNDATION.json names Yu Wang as the accountable publisher, curator, and rights holder and separately discloses AI drafting; no AI-agent label is used as the public author or rightsholder.
All seven Wikipedia articles are bound to exact page and revision IDs, revision timestamps and permanent URLs, raw API snapshots, extracted-text hashes, retrieval times, and word counts.

Every text source has pre-ingestion clearance bound to a retained and hashed Wikimedia Terms of Use snapshot; the same official snapshot was reused for four records after capture, with local-file retrieval honestly recorded.

Both NASA/JPL photographs are documented as U.S. federal government works in the public domain and have retained, hashed Commons description-page evidence. Six SVG diagrams are identified as original contributions by Yu Wang.

CC BY-SA 4.0 satisfies the adapted Wikipedia text obligation and is compatible with the public-domain photographs and original diagrams. The manifest, foundation, LICENSE, chapter notices, provenance, and attribution agree.

ATTRIBUTION.md mirrors structured provenance. Learner-facing chapters identify Wikipedia adaptation and the revision record; both photographs carry creator, source route, public-domain status, and modification status.

The independent pass verified the probability and entropy formulas, mutual-information identities, binary-symmetric-channel examples, capacity and noisy-channel theorem boundaries, code-rate calculations, distance guarantees, parity limitations, Hamming syndrome convention, extended-Hamming SECDED boundary, and interleaving limitation. No unresolved overclaim remains.

keyFacts.claim: For an outcome of positive probability p, self-information is -log_b(p); the logarithm base sets the unit, and the quantity measures statistical surprise rather than semantic importance.
keyFacts.note: Examples at probabilities 1/2, 1/4, and 1/8 recalculate to 1, 2, and 3 bits. The zero-probability boundary is treated as model-support failure, not infinite meaning.

keyFacts.claim: Discrete Shannon entropy is expected self-information and, for a fixed finite alphabet, is maximized by the uniform distribution.
keyFacts.note: The four-symbol distribution (1/2,1/4,1/8,1/8) gives 1.75 bits, below the 2-bit uniform maximum; the fixed-alphabet condition is stated.

keyFacts.claim: For discrete variables, mutual information can be expressed as H(X)-H(X|Y), is symmetric and nonnegative, and equals zero exactly at statistical independence.
keyFacts.note: The package does not infer causation from mutual information and labels the overlapping-region diagram as conceptual bookkeeping, not universal geometry.

keyFacts.claim: For a binary symmetric channel with crossover probability 0≤p≤1/2, a uniform input achieves capacity C=1-h2(p) bits per channel use.
keyFacts.note: At p=0.1 the recalculated binary entropy is approximately 0.469, giving approximately 0.531 bit/use; at p=1/2 capacity is zero. Every instance is scoped to the BSC model.

keyFacts.claim: Channel capacity maximizes mutual information over permitted input distributions; below capacity suitable code families can make error probability arbitrarily small asymptotically, whereas this does not guarantee zero error for a finite block or an arbitrary code.
keyFacts.note: The materials distinguish capacity from achieved mutual information and require code, block length, decoder, model validation, and finite-performance evidence for deployment claims.

keyFacts.claim: A binary block code mapping k message bits to n transmitted bits has rate k/n; redundancy restricts the valid transmitted strings but does not itself remove physical noise or determine minimum distance.
keyFacts.note: The (7,4) example gives rate 4/7, redundancy fraction 3/7, and expansion factor 7/4; those values are kept separate from correction capability.

keyFacts.claim: Under a hard-decision symbol-error model, minimum distance d_min guarantees detection of up to d_min-1 errors and correction of up to floor((d_min-1)/2) errors with an appropriate bounded-distance decoder.
keyFacts.note: The package explicitly distinguishes worst-case distance guarantees from average error probabilities and does not promise success outside the stated radius.

keyFacts.claim: A single even-parity check detects every odd number of bit flips but can miss an even number, so a passed check means only that this check detected no violation.
keyFacts.note: All learner-facing claims preserve the distinction between no detected error and no error.

keyFacts.claim: A standard binary Hamming (7,4) code has minimum distance 3 and supports guaranteed single-error correction; under the package's explicit parity-check convention, flipping position 4 of 0110011 yields received word 0111011 and syndrome 100.
keyFacts.note: The three displayed XOR checks were recalculated: 0110011 satisfies all checks, and only the position-4 check changes after the stated flip. Syndrome bit order is explicitly declared.

keyFacts.claim: Adding an overall parity bit can give an extended Hamming code minimum distance 4 for common SECDED use; this does not guarantee correction of every double error, and interleaving only rearranges a burst into separated errors for another code to handle.
keyFacts.note: The chapter consistently separates detection, correction, erasure, retransmission, and interleaving and avoids treating any one technique as universal reliability.
